mnemonics: COUNTING ALEPHS: Use 'Aleph' (ℵ) symbols to remember hierarchy: ℵ₀ (countable infinity, like natural numbers), ℵ₁ (next larger infinity, like real numbers). Think 'Aleph Null is Numerous, Aleph One is Uncountable'.
keyPoints: Cardinality measures the 'size' or number of elements in a set, even for infinite sets.
A set is countable if its elements can be put in one-to-one correspondence with natural numbers (e.g., integers, rationals).
The cardinality of the real numbers (continuum) is greater than that of the naturals; denoted c = 2^(ℵ₀) and is uncountable.
commonMistakes: Assuming all infinite sets have the same size (e.g., thinking naturals and reals are equally infinite).
Confusing countable infinity with uncountable infinity; e.g., assuming rationals are uncountable because they are dense, but they are actually countable.
